member.name: T:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Connectors.Ollama.OllamaPromptExecutionSettings
member.summary: Ollama Prompt Execution Settings.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Connectors.Ollama.OllamaPromptExecutionSettings.FromExecutionSettings(Microsoft.SemanticKernel.PromptExecutionSettings)
member.summary: Gets the specialization for the Ollama execution settings.
member.param: Generic prompt execution settings.

member.name: P:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Connectors.Ollama.OllamaPromptExecutionSettings.Stop
member.summary: Sets the stop sequences to use. When this pattern is encountered the LLM will stop generating text and return. Multiple stop patterns may be set by specifying multiple separate stop parameters in a model file.

member.name: P:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Connectors.Ollama.OllamaPromptExecutionSettings.TopK
member.summary: Reduces the probability of generating nonsense. A higher value (e.g. 100) will give more diverse answers, while a lower value (e.g. 10) will be more conservative. (Default: 40)

member.name: P:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Connectors.Ollama.OllamaPromptExecutionSettings.TopP
member.summary: Works together with top-k. A higher value (e.g., 0.95) will lead to more diverse text, while a lower value (e.g., 0.5) will generate more focused and conservative text. (Default: 0.9)

member.name: P:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Connectors.Ollama.OllamaPromptExecutionSettings.Temperature
member.summary: The temperature of the model. Increasing the temperature will make the model answer more creatively. (Default: 0.8)

member.name: P:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Connectors.Ollama.OllamaPromptExecutionSettings.NumPredict
member.summary: Maximum number of output tokens. (Default: -1, infinite generation)

member.name: T:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Diagnostics.ModelDiagnostics
member.summary: Model diagnostics helper class that provides a set of methods to trace model activities with the OTel semantic conventions. This class contains experimental features and may change in the future. To enable these features, set one of the following switches to true: `Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Experimental.GenAI.EnableOTelDiagnostics` `Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Experimental.GenAI.EnableOTelDiagnosticsSensitive` Or set the following environment variables to true: `SEMANTICKERNEL_EXPERIMENTAL_GENAI_ENABLE_OTEL_DIAGNOSTICS` `SEMANTICKERNEL_EXPERIMENTAL_GENAI_ENABLE_OTEL_DIAGNOSTICS_SENSITIVE`

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.KernelVerify.AllowedPluginNameSymbolsRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^[0-9A-Za-z_]*$
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match a character in the set [0-9A-Z_a-z] atomically any number of times.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.KernelVerify.AllowedFunctionNameSymbolsRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^[0-9A-Za-z_-]*$
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match a character in the set [-0-9A-Z_a-z] atomically any number of times.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Verify.FilenameRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^[^.]+\\.[^.]+$
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match a character other than '.' atomically at least once.
○ Match '.'.
○ Match a character other than '.' greedily at least once.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.name: T:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Text.ExceptionJsonConverter
member.summary: Serializes an exception as a string. This is useful when serializing an instance of an exception directly or indirectly via serializing an instance that references an exception. For example, when serializing chat history that contains FunctionCallContent or FunctionResultContent items referencing an exception. Serializing an exception without this converter will throw a System.NotSupportedException: Serialization and deserialization of System.Reflection.MethodBase instances is not supported. Path: $.Items.Exception.TargetSite.

members.member:
Caches common configurations of .\
All of the instances include a converter for . Once the System.Text.Json package is upgraded to 8.0+, this will no longer be necessary and the actual default can be used.

If you need to parse non-structured json streaming data, use instead.
SSE specification
This class is thread-safe.

member.name: T:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Text.StreamJsonParser
member.summary: Internal class for parsing a stream of text which contains a series of discrete JSON strings into en enumerable containing each separate JSON string.
member.remarks:
This is universal parser for parsing stream of text which contains a series of discrete JSON.
If you need a specialized SSE parser, use instead.
This class is thread-safe.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Text.StreamJsonParser.ParseAsync(System.IO.Stream,System.Boolean,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Parses a Stream containing JSON data and yields the individual JSON objects.
member.param: The Stream containing the JSON data.
Set to true to enable checking json chunks are well-formed. Default is false.
The cancellation token.
member.returns: An enumerable collection of string representing the individual JSON objects.
member.remarks: Stream will be disposed after parsing.
